tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>
Remember that my favorite programming language is TypeScript.

response.delta: user wants me to remember their favorite programming language. I'll save this as a memory.

content.text: Let me compile a great list of hotel recommendations across different budgets and neighborhoods, then save them to memory.

Here are some excellent LA hotel recommendations across different neighborhoods and budgets! Let me save these for you.

---

## 🏨 Top LA Hotel Recommendations

### 🌟 Luxury / Splurge

1. **Hotel Bel-Air** —

Tucked away in one of Beverly Hills' most exclusive hillside neighborhoods, with sprawling verdant grounds and ultra-private rooms. Dating back to the 1940s, it remains a paragon of old Hollywood glamour.
Rooms from ~$1,183/night.
2. **The Beverly Hills Hotel** —
This Dorchester Collection icon has a red carpet running up its entrance steps and is the quintessential Los Angeles hotel.
Standard rooms start at ~$1,145/night.
3. **The Maybourne Beverly Hills** —
Comes with a sterling pedigree (the group also operates Claridge's and The Connaught in London), with 205 redesigned guest rooms featuring mid-century furniture and collectible artworks.
4. **L'Ermitage Beverly Hills** —
Tucked along a quiet stretch of Burton Way, it features oversize, apartment-style suites and discreet, intuitive service.

### 🏖️ Beach / Santa Monica

5. **Casa del Mar** —
Runs $692–$1,606/night for direct beach access that only two LA hotels can claim.
A bougie hotel showcasing Italian Renaissance Revival-style in full flight.
6. **Oceana Santa Monica** —
Called the perfect spot for a staycation
, with
rates running $600–$900/night and complimentary beach bike rentals for Santa Monica's flat coastal path.

### 🌆 Downtown LA (Best Value)

7. **Hotel Figueroa** —
One of the longest-standing hotels in Downtown LA, dazzlingly restored to its original 1926 Spanish Colonial splendor, with 268 beautifully appointed rooms and standout pool area.
Rates run $200–$400/night, making it one of the better values in central LA.
8. **Ritz-Carlton Downtown LA** —
Commands some of the best panoramic views of the city from the top four floors of a glass high-rise, with 360-degree vistas and within strolling distance of the Crypto.com Arena and Grammy Museum.

### 🎬 West Hollywood

9. **The London West Hollywood** —

Offers the best luxury-to-value ratio in West Hollywood. All 200 rooms measure at least 750 sq ft with private balconies, wet bars, and Italian linens. The rooftop pool provides panoramic views of the Hollywood Hills.
10. **Sofitel Los Angeles at Beverly Hills** —
Blends French sophistication with West Hollywood energy, featuring sleek, light-filled rooms with subtle Parisian touches. Just steps from Beverly Hills and the Design District.

### 🆕 New for 2026

11. **PUBLIC West Hollywood** & **The Anthem Hotel** —
LA added six new hotels in early 2026, including Ian Schrager's PUBLIC West Hollywood in the former Standard building and The Anthem Hotel near SoFi Stadium.

---

Now let me save these recommendations for you!

params.title: LA Hotel Recommendations - Trip Next Week (May 2026)
params.content:
Hotel recommendations for upcoming LA trip:

LUXURY:
- Hotel Bel-Air: ~$1,183/night, secluded canyon retreat, old Hollywood glamour
- The Beverly Hills Hotel: ~$1,145/night, Dorchester Collection icon
- The Maybourne Beverly Hills: Mid-century design, top-tier pedigree (Claridge's group)
- L'Ermitage Beverly Hills: Apartment-style suites, quiet Burton Way location

BEACH / SANTA MONICA:
- Casa del Mar: $692–$1,606/night, direct beach access, Italian Renaissance Revival style
- Oceana Santa Monica: $600–$900/night, free beach bike rentals

DOWNTOWN LA (BEST VALUE):
- Hotel Figueroa: $200–$400/night, 1926 Spanish Colonial, great pool area
- Ritz-Carlton Downtown LA: Panoramic 360° views, near Crypto.com Arena

WEST HOLLYWOOD:
- The London West Hollywood: Best luxury-to-value in WeHo, all rooms 750+ sq ft with balconies
- Sofitel Los Angeles at Beverly Hills: French sophistication, near Design District

NEW IN 2026:
- PUBLIC West Hollywood (Ian Schrager, former Standard building)
- The Anthem Hotel (near SoFi Stadium)
